shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,_,W,_ : cmplog BLOCKS, value_profile_cmplog RESOLVES, value_profile & naive non-decisive. The only technique delta between the involved winner (vpc) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (both already carry the IDENTICAL I2S substitution mutator). Layer-1 attribution is therefore VP CMP_MAP gradient; this test is the INDEPENDENT real-data verification (G2) of HOW that gradient advantage manifests.
round0_refuted: Round-0 bet the whole shape on ONE story: VP's gradient closes scalar value-DISTANCE at the multi-byte gate operand (seed_set:value_distance_reached). The arbiter scored the inconclusive branches and NONE held: the VPC-resolving seed set does NOT sit at a smaller residual Hamming distance to a fixed gate operand than the cmplog-blocking set. The scalar-distance-closure mechanism is empirically REFUTED for this shape and is NOT re-proposed here.
round1_state: Round-1 pivoted to two BUILT corpus-state descriptors: H1 vp_enriches_size_tag_bytes (operand_enrichment, target-vs-decoy byte enrichment) and H2 vp_admits_size_tag_richer_corpus (joint_necessity analyze_pair, token/size lift). OUTCOME: H1 CONFIRMED exactly ONE branch -- libpng_4145 on sB (signed_target_enrich=14.974 > decoy_enrich=12.639). The other 6 branches did NOT hold: the harfbuzz pair (5474/5477, on-disk on s4) had BOTH H1 and H2 scored and NEITHER held; the libxml2 trio (6719/8100/9841, sB) had both scored and neither held; lcms_2090 (sB) had only H1 scorable (operand_enrichment returned no_gate_signature for the structural FOURCC switch) and it did not hold. So byte-enrichment and token/size-lift are NOT the VP advantage for these 6.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient enriches the VPC-resolving corpus for the target literal bytes relative to decoy bytes at the gate offset: near-miss byte matches register as new coverage, so the gradient persistently steers the saved corpus toward the exact contiguous literal that bare I2S substitution does not persistently enrich. CONFIRMED on libpng_4145 (sB).
hypotheses.frozen_note: KEEP verbatim (G-superset). This hypothesis already validated libpng_4145 on sB (signed_target_enrich 14.974 > decoy_enrich 12.639). Carried forward unchanged so that branch re-validates; it is NOT re-tested/relabelled/dropped. Its members list is the set of branches with a fixed-offset contiguous-literal gate the operand_enrichment descriptor applies to; the arbiter confirms whichever the byte-enrichment signal holds for (so far: libpng_4145) and lets the rest fall through to the new H3.

measurement.registry_tool_note: BUILT tool, descriptor_key corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio:target_vs_decoy:per_gate_offset. Each server runs it on its own on-disk targets (s4: harfbuzz; sB: libpng/lcms/libxml2). Returns no_gate_signature for the structural/switch gates (lcms_2090) -> those become not-scorable, correctly handled by the arbiter.

operands_by_branch.libpng_4145: target = ICC-signature printable-ASCII range bytes; decoys = non-signature payload bytes (00,b1,8f,0b,fc) from gAMA/iTXt seeds. CONFIRMED.
operands_by_branch.lcms_2090: target = 'Lab '/'XYZ ' FOURCC tokens (4c 61 62 20 / 58 59 5a 20); decoys = 'mntr'/'RGB '/'MCHF'. (operand_enrichment may return no_gate_signature on the switch dispatch -> then not scorable here, falls to H3.)
operands_by_branch.libxml2_6719: target = ':' (3a) + matched prefix bytes; decoys = '1' (31) version-string bytes.

hypotheses.prediction: signed_target_enrich > 0 AND signed_target_enrich > decoy_enrich: the VPC-resolving corpus is enriched for the target literal bytes relative to the decoy bytes at the gate offset. DISCRIMINATING: cmplog carries the IDENTICAL I2S mutator, so a pure-I2S byte-substitution advantage leaves no persistently near-miss-enriched corpus (signed_target_enrich ~ 0); a clearly positive value is the signature uniquely attributable to VP's gradient-driven admission of near-miss-then-exact corpus members. RULED OUT: the refuted scalar-distance story AND the pure-I2S story.

hypotheses.label: corroborant: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient drives corpus admission toward seeds that physically CONTAIN the target structural token (and toward larger seeds carrying the feature record), because the gradient rewards partial assembly that edge-only feedback discards. A token-availability / structural-assembly advantage, orthogonal to scalar distance and to byte enrichment.
hypotheses.frozen_note: KEEP verbatim (G-superset). Carried forward unchanged. analyze_pair(winner=value_profile_cmplog, loser=cmplog) mode -- L_W_ has NO plain value_profile blocking seeds, so the 3-arm i2s_necessary/vp_tags mode is N/A; the discriminating contrast is the W=VPC-resolving vs L=cmplog-blocking pair (winner_tags/loser_tags/tag_lift/size_lift).

measurement.registry_tool_note: BUILT tool, descriptor_key seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch, analyze_pair mode. No code change.

hypotheses.prediction: tag_lift = winner_tags - loser_tags >= 1.0 OR size_lift = winner_size/loser_size >= 1.3: the VPC-resolving seed set carries at least one MORE target token than the cmplog-blocking set, and/or is >=30% larger. DISCRIMINATING vs the refuted scalar-distance story (whole-token PRESENCE / assembly EXTENT, not residual Hamming distance) AND vs a pure-I2S story (identical I2S mutator -> tag_lift ~ 0 AND size_lift ~ 1.0). ALTERNATIVE RULED OUT: 'cmplog already assembles the same structure, just stalls on the final compare' -> tag_lift ~ 0 AND size_lift ~ 1.0.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round-2 pivot): value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient retains a corpus that drives the GATE LINE through MORE iterations / deeper into the loop per seed -- the VPC-resolving seeds re-enter the per-record sanitize / per-byte compare loop (harfbuzz FeatureList tag dispatch; libxml2 prefix-compare / xmlns cascade / DTD-lookahead loop) far more times than the cmplog-blocking seeds, so the branch line is EXECUTED more and the blocked side is actually TAKEN. This is an iteration-DEPTH / structural-traversal advantage measured by RUNNING the seeds through the cov image -- genuinely distinct from byte-enrichment (H1, which reads static corpus byte frequency) and from token/size counting (H2, which reads seed-set composition). It explains why H1+H2 failed on these branches: the VPC corpus is not necessarily byte-enriched for the exact literal nor token-richer, but it REACHES the gate line many more times, giving the gradient repeated near-miss credit the loser corpus never accrues.
hypotheses.novelty_note: G1-novel + genuinely-different mechanism. The two refuted round-1 stories are STATIC corpus reads (byte_freq_ratio, struct/token count); this RUNS the W/L seed sets through libafl-<target>-cov and reads the branch-line execution count. The untried built tool the brief flagged ('depth_reach ... NOT yet tried here'). A seed can be neither byte-enriched (H1 fails) nor token-richer (H2 fails) yet still drive the sanitize/compare LOOP through more iterations -- depth and presence/enrichment are orthogonal axes, which is exactly why depth can hold after both static reads failed.

measurement.registry_tool_note: BUILT tool, descriptor_key seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch. Run with --winner-fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --loser-fuzzer cmplog (the involved winner/loser of this shape, NOT its ctx/ngram anchor pair). Reads winner_line_count vs loser_line_count at the gate line from the llvm-cov dump after pushing the W/L seed sets through the cov image. No code change -- the branch entry point + --winner/--loser flags already exist. Needs the target's libafl-<target>-cov image (present: libafl-harfbuzz-cov on s4) and >=1 W + >=1 L seed in DB; each server runs only its on-disk targets.

branch_line_by_branch.harfbuzz_5474: hb-ot-layout-common.hh:688 (FeatureParams::sanitize tag dispatch; per FeatureList record).
branch_line_by_branch.harfbuzz_5477: hb-ot-layout-common.hh:812 (OT::Feature::sanitize closure tag; per Feature record).
branch_line_by_branch.libxml2_9841: valid.c:4660 reached via the xmlns equality cascade (L1235/L1724); count the cascade compare line executions.

params.note: single per-branch Docker run ~30-60s. lcms_2090 EXCLUDED -- it is a one-shot switch arm (AddConversion line 458), not a loop, so there is no iteration-depth signal to read; it is honestly decidable:false (H4).

hypotheses.prediction: depth_lift = winner_line_count / max(loser_line_count,1) >> 1 AND winner_deeper = true (the blocked side is taken by the W corpus, not the L corpus): the VPC-resolving corpus drives the gate line through many more executions per seed than the cmplog-blocking corpus. DISCRIMINATING: an alternative in which the two corpora reach the gate line equally often and the VP advantage is purely the final-compare substitution (pure-I2S, refuted) predicts depth_lift ~ 1.0 AND winner_deeper false. A second alternative -- the advantage is static byte-enrichment / token-presence (H1/H2, already refuted for these branches) -- is orthogonal to and does NOT predict a per-seed execution-count lift. So a clear depth_lift>>1 with winner_deeper is the signature uniquely attributable to VP's gradient RETAINING deeper-traversing corpus members. G2-INDEPENDENT: the line execution count is read off the cov dump of each arm's CORPUS, computed without reference to the vpc-resolves label that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: honest non-discriminable (G3): lcms_2090 gates on a one-shot color-space SWITCH ARM (AddConversion line 458, Lab->XYZ PCS selection), NOT a loop and NOT a clean fixed-offset contiguous literal -- operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature on the switch dispatch (confirmed: only 1/2 rules were scorable on sB and it did not hold), joint_necessity found no token/size lift, the scalar-distance story is refuted shape-wide, and there is no iteration-depth signal to read (single-shot switch, so depth_reach has no loop to count). No BUILT measurement discriminates VP's mechanism for this branch on EITHER server. It carries the Layer-1 VP-gradient label but gets NO independent confirmation; the arbiter correctly leaves it inconclusive (fallback). This is an honest no-clean-test outcome, not a label dodged off-server -- it is undecidable even on sB which owns the lcms corpus.
hypotheses.prediction: N/A -- no built measurement discriminates a single-shot FOURCC switch arm (operand_enrichment no_gate_signature; joint_necessity no lift; value_distance refuted; depth_reach has no loop). The VPC-resolving advantage at a one-time switch-case selection of the Lab/XYZ PCS pair is genuinely below the resolution of any current corpus/seed/cov tool.
hypotheses.rule: decidable:false -> inconclusive (fallback). Whichever server owns the lcms corpus (sB) reaches the SAME undecidable verdict; do not chase a label here.

data_realities_respected: value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> NEVER measured via a seed_lineage 'gradient event'. Its effect is read off the corpus the gradient ADMITTED -- byte enrichment (operand_enrichment), structural size+token count (joint_necessity), and now per-seed gate-line execution depth (depth_reach, which RUNS the admitted corpus through the cov image). All three are corpus/seed-state computes, no lineage event.
I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an I2S event proposed.

depth_reach RUNS seeds through libafl-<target>-cov; it is used here with --winner-fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --loser-fuzzer cmplog (the involved VP-pro pair), NOT its ctx/ngram anchor pair -- the 'winner corpus reaches the line deeper' compute is fuzzer-pair-agnostic, so it validly reads the VPC-vs-cmplog depth contrast. It needs the target cov image (present for harfbuzz on s4) + >=1 W and >=1 L seed in DB; each server runs only its on-disk targets.

on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3 on s4 and lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty on sB -> the harfbuzz pair is seed/depth-measurable on s4; the lcms/libxml2 branches are measurable on sB which owns them. Cross-server: NO branch is marked decidable:false merely for absent corpus on the running server -- the owning server arbitrates its own targets with these same descriptors.
L_W_ has NO plain value_profile arm in the corpus (vp is non-decisive) -> joint_necessity is used in analyze_pair(VPC vs cmplog) mode, NOT its 3-arm i2s_necessary mode.
depth_reach is BLIND to a one-shot (non-loop) gate -> lcms_2090's single switch arm has no iteration-depth signal, so it is honestly decidable:false (H4) rather than forced into a depth rule.
notes:
PRIORS KEPT (superset/freeze, verbatim): H1 vp_enriches_size_tag_bytes (operand_enrichment) -- CONFIRMED libpng_4145 on sB (signed_target_enrich 14.974 > decoy_enrich 12.639); and H2 vp_admits_size_tag_richer_corpus (joint_necessity, analyze_pair). Both retained unchanged so the confirmed branch re-validates; no relaxed-threshold re-test.

NEW MECHANISM (round-2): H3 vp_corpus_drives_sanitize_loop_deeper, via the BUILT depth_reach tool (seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch) -- the brief's flagged untried pivot. It is a GENUINELY different mechanism from the two refuted static reads: H1/H2 read static corpus byte frequency + seed-set token/size composition; H3 RUNS the W/L seeds through libafl-<target>-cov and reads the gate line's per-arm execution count, capturing whether the VPC corpus drives the per-record sanitize loop (harfbuzz) / per-byte prefix-compare + xmlns cascade + DTD-lookahead loop (libxml2) through MORE iterations -- giving the gradient repeated near-miss credit the loser corpus never accrues. This DIRECTLY explains why H1+H2 failed on the harfbuzz pair (BOTH scored, neither held) and the libxml2 trio: the VPC advantage is not byte-enrichment nor token-richness but iteration-DEPTH, an orthogonal axis. G1: predicts depth_lift>>1 + winner_deeper; the pure-I2S and the byte-enrichment alternatives predict depth_lift~1. G2: the line count is read off each arm's corpus cov dump, independent of the resolve label.

H3 MEMBERS: harfbuzz_5474, harfbuzz_5477 (s4 on-disk, libafl-harfbuzz-cov present, 11 W / 18 L seeds each), and libxml2_6719/8100/9841 (sB on-disk -- loop-driven gates: prefix-compare loop, DTD-lookahead loop, xmlns equality cascade -- sB runs depth_reach on its libafl-libxml2-cov image). lcms_2090 is EXCLUDED (one-shot switch, no loop).

DECIDABLE:FALSE (G3, honest): H4 vp_advantage_switch_arm_undecidable covers lcms_2090 ONLY. It is a single-shot color-space SWITCH arm (AddConversion line 458), not a loop and not a clean fixed-offset contiguous literal: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (confirmed -- only 1/2 rules scorable on sB), joint_necessity found no lift, the scalar-distance story is refuted, and there is no iteration to count for depth_reach. No built measurement discriminates VP's mechanism here on EITHER server -- an honest no-clean-test outcome, NOT an off-server dodge (sB owns lcms and reaches the same undecidable verdict).

NO NEW DESCRIPTOR NEEDED: all three decidable hypotheses reuse BUILT registry tools by descriptor_key (operand_enrichment, joint_necessity, depth_reach). No code change.

EXPECTED OUTCOME: libpng_4145 re-validates under frozen H1. The harfbuzz pair (same 'size' mechanism at two adjacent sanitize sites, identical seed sets) should co-resolve under H3 if the VPC corpus drives the FeatureList sanitize dispatch deeper per seed; if depth_lift~1 there too, H3 is correctly refuted for them and they fall to inconclusive (do not chase 100%). The libxml2 loop-gates are H3's strongest structural fit (explicit iteration). lcms_2090 stays honestly inconclusive.
